genre_refs: Howard Chandler Christy 'Scene at the Signing of the Constitution of the United States' (1940 painting)
description: A clean reproduction of Howard Chandler Christy's famous 1940 history painting 'Scene at the Signing of the Constitution of the United States', shared by the Department of Labor as a patriotic Constitution Day card. The oil painting depicts the interior of Independence Hall in warm brown and gold tones, with a large brass chandelier overhead and tall draped windows. George Washington stands at right on a raised dais beside an American flag, presiding over several dozen Founding Fathers in 18th-century dress (coats, breeches, white stockings); Benjamin Franklin is seated centrally in the foreground while groups of delegates converse around green-cloth-covered tables. The image itself carries no overlaid graphic text, slogan, or agency seal; it is a straight art reproduction posted to mark a federal observance. It functions as a designed seasonal/holiday commemorative graphic rather than a candid photo. Messaging technique is appeal to patriotism, founding-era heritage, and reverence for the Constitution; the tweet text supplies the framing ('Happy Constitution Day, Patriots!'). Target audience is a patriotic general public. There is no on-image text.
notable_text: (no on-image text; tweet caption: 'Happy Constitution Day, Patriots!')
